Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the mortgage LOS for Amy Chen's assigned loans using mortgage_los_search_loans (query "Amy Chen" or similar) or mortgage_los_get_pipeline (filtered by assigned_to using Amy's staff ID los_staff_4d01ebef3a15). This returns Amy's active loan portfolio including LN-2026-00628 (Larry Harrison, underwriting), LN-2026-00625 (Katie Morris, self-employed borrower, underwriting), LN-2026-00211 (Tiffany Brooks, processing), LN-2026-00607 (Lauren Bennett, processing), and LN-2026-00615 (Marisol Reyes, processing).
OE 2: Retrieve conditions for loan LN-2026-00628 (Harrison) using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_3a7f88229103"). The prompt explicitly asks to "pull the loan conditions too" to check if anything else is outstanding on the Harrison file beyond the income issue. This returns 5 conditions: 3 outstanding (4506-T income verification, updated pay stub within 30 days, homeowners insurance evidence) and 2 cleared (bank statements, appraisal at $463,970).
OE 3: Search emails with a query related to "Harrison" or "IRS transcript" or "LN-2026-00628" (or similar) using search_emails to find Elena Marchetti's email to Amy proposing a restructure to approximately $245K loan amount using the IRS transcript income figure of $72K, noting the lender (UWM) will accept the transcript figure as qualifying income, and that Elena has NOT told Harrison any of this yet because she wanted to confirm with Amy first. Also finds Amy's earlier SENT email to Larry Harrison outlining options based on the 4506-T transcript showing $72,000.
OE 4: Search Slack with a query related to "Harrison" or "4506-T" or "72k" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Elena's messages in #loan-processing (channel C002): "Anyone ever had a 4506-T come back this far off? Larry Harrison file is 72k on transcript vs 95k on the returns in file. About a 23k gap." and the follow-up: "Larry just came back hot on LN-2026-00628. Says his filed returns are right, IRS transcript is wrong, and doesn't want us touching loan amt. Amy looping you in before this escalates more."
OE 5: Search Slack with a query related to "QC" or "condition clearance" or "Tyler" or "cleared" or "Raymond" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Raymond's message in #loan-processing (channel C002) about 3 files where conditions show cleared in LOS but supporting PDFs are missing from doc folders, Grace Yamamoto's response about needing Raymond in her office and scheduling a QC follow-up ("Raymond/Denise - I put 9:30 on tomorrow for QC follow-up"), and Raymond's DM to Amy in channel D_AMY_RAYMOND: "Hey Amy, heads up — one of your files came up in my QC review. The Tiffany Brooks loan, LN-2026-00211. Found some condition clearances in LOS that don't have the supporting docs behind them. Wasn't your doing — looks like Tyler cleared them — but you should know since it's your file."
OE 6: Search emails with a query related to "QC exceptions" or "Tyler-cleared" or "Raymond" (or similar) using search_emails to find Raymond Chen's formal QC email to Grace Yamamoto (subject: "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files"): three exception loans where conditions were marked cleared without supporting docs, including LN-2026-00603 (Heather Sullivan, closed — most concerning due to post-close liability), LN-2026-00211 (open), and LN-2026-00224 (open, same pattern), with the LOS audit trail showing Tyler Washington clearing the conditions on all three.
OE 7: Retrieve conditions for loan LN-2026-00211 (Tiffany Brooks) using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d") to verify which conditions were cleared without supporting documentation per the QC finding. Returns 4 conditions, all with status "cleared" and cleared_by "los_staff_25ab76814149" (Tyler Washington): bank statements, pay stub, appraisal at $235,262, and homeowners insurance.
OE 8: Search Slack with a query related to "self-employed" or "self-emp" or "180k" or "48k" or "write-offs" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Elena's message in #loan-processing (channel C002): "Amy, reviewed the self-emp docs on this file. 2025 returns show ~180k gross rev but qualifying income is only landing around 48k after write-offs."
OE 9: Browse CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (without contact/company filter, since the key engagement eng_inject_001 has no contact linkage) or crm_show_data to discover the engagement note titled "LN-2026-00625 income qualification — needs resolution" documenting that Marcus Webb suggested a bank statement loan program through Plaza Home Mortgage as an alternative to conventional qualifying, using 12 months of deposits instead of tax returns. Marcus was going to check Plaza guidelines but gave notice before following through, leaving the file stalled with no active resolution path.
OE 10: Look up Amy Chen's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Amy Chen") to retrieve amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com before sending the summary email.
OE 11: Add a loan activity note via mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_3a7f88229103", action and detail required) to loan LN-2026-00628 (Harrison) documenting the IRS transcript discrepancy ($72K transcript vs $95K on returns), Elena's proposed restructure to approximately $245K using the transcript income, and that the borrower has not yet been informed of this option.
OE 12: Add a loan activity note via mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d", action and detail required) to loan LN-2026-00211 (Tiffany Brooks) documenting the QC finding that Tyler Washington (los_staff_25ab76814149) cleared all 4 conditions without supporting documentation in the file, and the remediation needed.
OE 13: Add a loan activity note via mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_7163cb114daf", action and detail required) to loan LN-2026-00625 (Katie Morris, self-employed borrower) documenting the qualifying income gap (~$180K gross vs ~$48K net after write-offs), Marcus Webb's suggested bank statement program via Plaza Home Mortgage, Marcus's departure before following through, and the unresolved status.
OE 14: Send an email via send_email (sender and recipients required) to amy.chen@keystonemortgage.com with the full breakdown of all three files: Harrison IRS transcript status ($72K vs $95K), Elena's restructure proposal (~$245K), and that Harrison hasn't been told yet; the QC finding on LN-2026-00211 identifying Tiffany Brooks as the file and Tyler Washington as the person who cleared conditions without supporting docs; and the self-employed borrower's (LN-2026-00625) stalled income qualification after Marcus Webb's departure, with the bank statement program path unresolved.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 The model must use a mortgage LOS tool (e.g., mortgage_los_search_loans, mortgage_los_get_pipeline, or mortgage_los_get_conditions) to look up Amy Chen's loan files.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used multiple mortgage LOS tools: mortgage_los_search_loans (querying 'Harrison', 'LN-2026-00628', 'LN-2026-00211'), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (with assigned_to filter), mortgage_los_get_conditions, mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, and mortgage_los_add_activity. LOS tools were clearly used to look up Amy Chen's loan files.
✓Run #2The agent called multiple mortgage LOS tools including mortgage_los_search_loans (searching 'Harrison' and 'LN-2026-00628'), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (with assigned_to filter), mortgage_los_get_conditions (multiple loan IDs), and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (multiple loan IDs). The LOS was clearly queried as a source of truth.
✓Run #3The agent used multiple mortgage LOS tools including mortgage_los_search_loans (querying 'Harrison', 'LN-2026-00603', 'Bhandari'), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (with assigned_to filter), mortgage_los_get_conditions (on multiple loan IDs), and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id multiple times. These reads from the LOS are clearly present in the trajectory.
✓Run #4The agent called mortgage_los_search_loans (querying 'Harrison' and 'Larry Harrison'), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (with assigned_to filter), mortgage_los_get_conditions, mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, and other mortgage_los_* tools throughout the trajectory.
✓Run #5The agent called multiple mortgage LOS tools including mortgage_los_search_loans (for 'Harrison', 'LN-2026-00628', 'LN-2026-00603'), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (with assigned_to filter), mortgage_los_get_conditions, and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id throughout the trajectory.
✓Run #6The agent called multiple mortgage LOS tools throughout the trajectory: mortgage_los_search_borrowers (for Harrison, Brooks, Morris, Gonzalez), mortgage_los_get_pipeline (with assigned_to filter), mortgage_los_get_conditions (multiple loan IDs), mortgage_los_get_activity_log, mortgage_los_get_documents, mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id, and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id.
2 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_list_engagements, crm_search_contacts, or crm_search_deals) to look up engagement notes or deal records related to Amy's loan files.
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1There are no CRM tool calls (crm_list_engagements, crm_search_contacts, crm_search_deals, or any crm_* tool) anywhere in the trajectory. The agent relied exclusively on Slack message searches and email searches, never querying the CRM system.
✗Run #2The agent used contacts_search_contacts (not a crm_* tool) to look up Camille and Raymond, but never called crm_list_engagements, crm_search_contacts, or crm_search_deals. No CRM engagement notes were retrieved. As a result, the agent missed Marcus Webb's CRM engagement note about the Plaza Home Mortgage bank statement program suggestion for LN-2026-00625, instead incorrectly stating 'I couldn't find direct evidence of Marcus Webb working this file.'
✗Run #3The agent used contacts_search_contacts (not a CRM engagement tool) to look up Raymond, but never called crm_list_engagements, crm_search_contacts, or crm_search_deals. Without checking CRM engagement notes, the agent missed the CRM record where Marcus Webb's bank statement loan suggestion via Plaza Home Mortgage was documented.
✗Run #4No CRM tool calls (crm_list_engagements, crm_search_contacts, crm_search_deals, or similar) appear anywhere in the trajectory. The agent used contacts_search_contacts (an LOS-adjacent tool) but no CRM-specific tools.
✗Run #5The agent used contacts_search_contacts (for Raymond, Denise, Tyler) but never called any crm_* tool such as crm_list_engagements, crm_search_contacts, or crm_search_deals. No CRM engagement notes were queried, meaning the Marcus Webb bank statement program suggestion via Plaza Home Mortgage was not discovered from CRM.
✗Run #6The agent never called any crm_* tool (crm_list_engagements, crm_search_contacts, crm_search_deals). The only contact-related tool called was contacts_search_contacts with query 'Raymond', which is a different tool. No CRM engagement notes were queried, meaning the agent could not have found Marcus Webb's suggestion of the bank statement program via Plaza Home Mortgage.
3 The model must pass a query related to 'Amy Chen' or 'Amy' (or similar), or filter by her staff ID (los_staff_4d01ebef3a15), when calling a mortgage LOS tool to retrieve her assigned loans.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline with the parameter assigned_to: 'los_staff_4d01ebef3a15', which is Amy Chen's staff ID. This correctly scoped the pipeline query to Amy's files.
✓Run #2The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline with the parameter assigned_to: 'los_staff_4d01ebef3a15', which is Amy Chen's staff ID, correctly scoping the pipeline query to her loans.
✓Run #3The agent logged in as Amy Chen and obtained her staff ID (los_staff_4d01ebef3a15), then called mortgage_los_get_pipeline with assigned_to: 'los_staff_4d01ebef3a15'. This correctly filtered the pipeline to Amy's loans.
✓Run #4The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline with input {'assigned_to': 'los_staff_4d01ebef3a15'}, which is Amy Chen's staff ID, explicitly filtering the pipeline to her assigned loans.
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline with the parameter assigned_to: 'los_staff_4d01ebef3a15', which is Amy Chen's staff ID, correctly scoping the pipeline query to her files.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_get_pipeline with parameter assigned_to: 'los_staff_4d01ebef3a15', which is Amy Chen's staff ID, effectively filtering the pipeline to her assigned loans.
4 The model must pass a query related to 'QC,' 'condition clearance,' 'Tyler,' 'cleared,' or 'Raymond' (or similar) when calling conversations_search_messages to find information about the QC review finding.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent made multiple conversations_search_messages calls with relevant QC-related queries including 'Raymond QC review condition cleared', 'QC review', 'condition cleared docs', 'Tyler cleared condition', 'Raymond', 'QC condition cleared supporting', 'quality control flagged', and 'Tyler QC file risk processing'. These targeted searches successfully found Raymond's Slack message about the QC finding.
✓Run #2The agent made numerous conversations_search_messages calls with QC-related terms including 'Raymond QC review condition cleared', 'QC review condition', 'QC cleared condition docs', 'condition cleared without supporting', 'QC audit', 'Tyler processing', 'QC', 'Tyler cleared condition Brooks', and 'Tiffany Brooks'. The search for 'Tiffany Brooks' returned the critical Slack message from Raymond about the QC finding.
